perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Non-Competition . For a period of three (3) years from and after the Effective Date (the “ Restrictive Period ”), Sellers shall not engage, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any person (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise), in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer during the Restrictive Period. The foregoing restrictions shall, however, apply only for a one-year period from and after the Effective Date with respect to any successor of any Seller who is not engaged in the same type of business as the Business as of the Effective Date and shall not apply at all with respect to any successor, other than Sellers and any of their Affiliates as of the Effective Date, that is engaged in the same type of business as the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Non-Competition . For a period of three (3) years from and after the Effective Date (the “ Restrictive Period ”), Sellers shall not engage, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any person (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise), in any business activity which is in competition with the Business conducted by Buyer during the Restrictive Period. The foregoing restrictions shall, however, apply only for a five-year period from and after the Effective Date with respect to any successor of any Seller who is not engaged in the same type of business as the Business as of the Effective Date and shall not apply at all with respect to any successor, other than Sellers and any of their Affiliates as of the Effective Date, that is engaged in the same type of business as the Business.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the non-competition clause, altering the restrictive period applicable to successors of the seller not engaged in the same type of business from one year to five years. This contradicts with section 1.5, which acknowledges that the covenants are reasonable in all respects, as a dramatic change in the duration may render the agreement unreasonable and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Reasonableness of Covenants . Sellers acknowledge and agree that the covenants contained in this Agreement are reasonable in all respects, including without limitation their duration and geographic scope. Sellers expressly waive any defenses as to the reasonableness of such covenants in any action between the parties to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the restrictive period from one year to five years for successors of the seller not engaged in the same type of business. This directly contradicts section 1.5, which states that all covenants, including their duration, are reasonable. Increasing the duration of the non-competition agreement from one year to five years could render the agreement unreasonable, thus creating a strong inconsistency and a potential unenforceability issue, fulfilling the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Non-Solicitation . During the Restrictive Period, without the prior written consent of the Buyer (which Buyer may withhold in the exercise of its sole, absolute, and arbitrary discretion), Sellers (including, without limitation, any successors) shall not induce or solicit, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any other person, (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise) any employee of the Buyer or its Affiliates who is involved in the managed modem business conducted by Buyer or any of its Affiliates, including, without limitation, the Business, to terminate the employee’s employment with the Buyer or any such Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Non-Solicitation . During the Restrictive Period, without the prior written consent of the Buyer (which Buyer may arbitrarily deny), Sellers (including, without limitation, any successors) shall not induce or solicit, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any other person, (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise) any employee of the Buyer or its Affiliates who is involved in the managed modem business conducted by Buyer or any of its Affiliates, including, without limitation, the Business, to terminate the employee’s employment with the Buyer or any such Affiliate.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Buyer may withhold in the exercise of its sole, absolute, and arbitrary discretion" to "Buyer may arbitrarily deny", we introduce ambiguity about the buyer's discretion, which contradicts the original intent that the buyer can deny the consent for any reason or no reason. This contradiction relates to section 1.5 (Reasonableness of Covenants) because an absolute denial by the Buyer could be argued as unreasonable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 Reasonableness of Covenants . Sellers acknowledge and agree that the covenants contained in this Agreement are reasonable in all respects, including without limitation their duration and geographic scope. Sellers expressly waive any defenses as to the reasonableness of such covenants in any action between the parties to enforce this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Buyer may withhold consent in its 'sole, absolute, and arbitrary discretion'. The changed text modifies this to 'Buyer may arbitrarily deny'. This weakens the explicitly stated discretion, creating ambiguity as to whether the denial must be reasonable or not. This contradiction directly impacts Section 1.5 (Reasonableness of Covenants), because the original version explicitly allows the buyer to deny for any reason, or no reason, which the sellers agree is reasonable. The modified version opens up the possibility that an arbitrary denial could be deemed unreasonable, which introduces an in-text contradiction. Thus, it adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.6 Certain Definitions . As used in this Agreement: 2 (a) “Affiliate” means, with respect to any Person, a Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controls, is Controlled by, or is under common Control with, such Person; provided that a Person will not be deemed to Control another Person if the only indicia of such Control is voting control of more than 10% but less than 20% of outstanding, publicly traded equity securities of such other Person.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 Certain Definitions . As used in this Agreement: 2 (a) “Affiliate” means, with respect to any Person, a Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controls, is Controlled by, or is under common Control with, such Person; provided that a Person will not be deemed to Control another Person if the only indicia of such Control is voting control of more than 30% but less than 40% of outstanding, publicly traded equity securities of such other Person.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the definition of Affiliate by adjusting the voting control percentage. Originally, control was defined between 10% and 20%, but now it's between 30% and 40%. This change creates a direct in-text contradiction, as other sections rely on the original definition of 'Affiliate' for non-solicitation (1.3) and non-competition (1.2) obligations. For instance, section 1.3 says "any employee of the Buyer or its Affiliates who is involved in the managed modem business", the changed defintion would impact who is considered an affiliate and therefore the non-solicitation clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Non-Solicitation . During the Restrictive Period, without the prior written consent of the Buyer (which Buyer may arbitrarily deny), Sellers (including, without limitation, any successors) shall not induce or solicit, directly or indirectly, through any other entity or with any other person, (whether as an owner, shareholder, partner, member, director, officer, employee, agent, investor, or otherwise) any employee of the Buyer or its Affiliates who is involved in the managed modem business conducted by Buyer or any of its Affiliates, including, without limitation, the Business, to terminate the employee’s employment with the Buyer or any such Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Affiliate' by increasing the percentage of voting control required to be considered an affiliate (from 10-20% to 30-40%). This directly contradicts section 1.3 (Non-Solicitation), which refers to employees of the Buyer or its Affiliates. Because the definition of affiliate has been changed, the scope of the non-solicitation clause changes impacting the entities and people it covers. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, thus, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Applicable Law, Jurisdiction, and Venue . This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Colorado applicable to agreements made and to be performed wholly within such jurisdiction. The parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Colorado and of the United States of America, in each case located in the County of Denver, for any litigation arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to its respective address set forth in this Agreement shall be effective service of process for any litigation brought against it in any such court. The parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive any objection to the laying of venue of any litigation arising out of this Agreement in the courts of the State of Colorado or the United States of America, in each case, located in the County of Denver, and hereby further 3 irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agree not to plead or claim in any such court that any such litigation brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Applicable Law, Jurisdiction, and Venue . This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of New York applicable to agreements made and to be performed wholly within such jurisdiction. The parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Colorado and of the United States of America, in each case located in the County of Denver, for any litigation arising out of or relating to this Agreement, and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to its respective address set forth in this Agreement shall be effective service of process for any litigation brought against it in any such court. The parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally waive any objection to the laying of venue of any litigation arising out of this Agreement in the courts of the State of Colorado or the United States of America, in each case, located in the County of Denver, and hereby further 3 irrevocably and unconditionally waive and agree not to plead or claim in any such court that any such litigation brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the governing law from Colorado to New York, and the jurisdiction from exclusive to non-exclusive, we create a direct in-text contradiction. Section 2.4 (Attorney Fees) states that if any action is brought to enforce the terms, the prevailing party is entitled to attorney's fees. The applicable law now being New York's, the interpretation and enforceability will now be subject to New York Law not Colorado, this creates an in-text contradiction about which Law to apply when deciding the Attorney fees clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Attorney Fees . If any action at law or in equity is brought to enforce or interpret the terms of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to its reasonable attorneys’ fees, costs, expert witness fees and all other disbursements in addition to any other relief to which it or he may be entitled.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from Colorado to New York and changes the jurisdiction from exclusive to non-exclusive. Section 2.4 (Attorney Fees) states that if any action is brought to enforce the terms, the prevailing party is entitled to attorney's fees. The applicable law is now New York's, so the interpretation and enforceability will now be subject to New York Law, not Colorado law. This creates a contradiction about which law applies when deciding the attorney's fees clause, as outlined in the Perturbation Explanation and adheres to the Inconsistencies definition, triggering a strong in-text contradiction with Section 2.4.

perturbation.original_text: 2.9 Entire Agreement . This Agreement and the Purchase Agreement and the documents referred to therein constitute the entire agreement among the parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersede all prior agreements, letters of intent, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties, whether oral or written.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.9 Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes a partial agreement among the parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof and does not necessarily supersede all prior agreements, letters of intent, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties, whether oral or written.
perturbation.explanation: By stating this agreement is a "partial agreement", there's a contradiction. Section 2.5 (Modification) states that the agreement may not be amended except by written agreement, this contradicts the idea that there can be other, non-written parts of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5 Modification . This Agreement may not be amended or modified by the parties except by a written agreement executed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 2.9 to state that the agreement is a "partial agreement", implying that other agreements, potentially unwritten, could exist. This directly contradicts Section 2.5, which explicitly states that the agreement can only be amended or modified by a written agreement executed by both parties. The change creates an inconsistency because it suggests the existence of non-written agreements that could modify or amend the main agreement, which is not allowed under Section 2.5. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.11 Notice . Any notice required or permitted to be given hereunder shall be sufficient if in writing and if hand delivered, sent by overnight courier, or sent by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, addressed as follows: If to the Buyer: Level 3 Communications, LLC 1025 Eldorado Blvd. Broomfield, Colorado 80021 Attention: General Counsel With a copy to: Otten, Johnson, Robinson, Neff & Ragonetti, P.C. 1600 U.S. Bank Tower 950 Seventeenth Street Denver, Colorado 80202 Attention: Steven E. Segal, Esq. If to Sellers: ICG Communications, Inc. 161 Inverness Drive West Englewood, Colorado 80112 Attention: General Counsel [Signatures on following page]
perturbation.changed_text: 2.11 Notice . Any notice required or permitted to be given hereunder shall be sufficient if in writing or verbally and if hand delivered, sent by overnight courier, or sent by registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, addressed as follows: If to the Buyer: Level 3 Communications, LLC 1025 Eldorado Blvd. Broomfield, Colorado 80021 Attention: General Counsel With a copy to: Otten, Johnson, Robinson, Neff & Ragonetti, P.C. 1600 U.S. Bank Tower 950 Seventeenth Street Denver, Colorado 80202 Attention: Steven E. Segal, Esq. If to Sellers: ICG Communications, Inc. 161 Inverness Drive West Englewood, Colorado 80112 Attention: General Counsel [Signatures on following page]
perturbation.explanation: By permitting the notice to be 'verbal', this contradicts with section 2.5 Modification, which specifically stipulates that any modifications or amendments must be in "written agreement executed by both parties". Allowing verbal notice creates in-text contradiction about the agreement can be updated without having written notice
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5 Modification . This Agreement may not be amended or modified by the parties except by a written agreement executed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 2.11 to allow 'verbal' notice, which directly contradicts section 2.5 that requires all modifications to the agreement to be in 'written agreement'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the agreement can be modified through verbal agreements, as described in 2.11, despite section 2.5 stating that they can only be modified through written agreements. This creates a strong contradiction and uncertainty about the enforceability of modifications to the original contract.
